key_insight: SIGMA improves multi-agent performance by composing agents from reusable skills, then learning the communication graph over those constructed agents instead of treating agent nodes as fixed entities.
engineering_relevance: For computational science and engineering workflows, this suggests a more scalable way to assemble task-specific agent teams that can generalize to new capability combinations and adapt communication to the task.
method: Predict a skill-agent incidence matrix from the task and a skill library.
Build each agent embedding by composing the selected skills.
Decode a communication topology and route messages through skill-specific mailboxes.
evidence_signal: Across six reasoning and coding benchmarks with three base LLMs, SIGMA achieved the best average performance and outperformed CARD by 2.06, 2.36, and 1.75 points on the three base models, with only a 0.96-point average drop under unseen skill libraries.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and coverage of the skill library, so incomplete or poorly defined skills could limit compositional benefits in practice.
paper_title: SIGMA: Skill-Incidence Graphs for Compositional Multi-Agent Design
